perturbation.original_text: You (which term shall include your subsidiaries or other entities controlled by you) have requested information regarding Zeigler Coal Holding Company (which term, together with its subsidiaries or other controlled entities, the "Company", "us" or "we") in connection with your consideration of the possible acquisition of the Company (a "Possible Transaction"). In consideration of our furnishing you with the Evaluation Materials (as defined below) you agree as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: You (which term shall include only your direct subsidiaries) have requested information regarding Zeigler Coal Holding Company (which term, together with its subsidiaries or other controlled entities, the "Company", "us" or "we") in connection with your consideration of the possible acquisition of the Company (a "Possible Transaction"). In consideration of our furnishing you with the Evaluation Materials (as defined below) you agree as follows:
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'You' is changed to include 'only your direct subsidiaries' instead of all subsidiaries or other entities controlled by you. This introduces a contradiction because later sections refer to the original broader definition of 'You' when discussing responsibilities and liabilities, potentially excluding indirect subsidiaries from these obligations. The modified definition creates an ambiguity regarding which entities are bound by the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your representatives. In the event that you or any of your representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, you or your representatives as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist, or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.justification: The change in the definition of 'You' from including 'your subsidiaries or other entities controlled by you' to 'only your direct subsidiaries' creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text'. The contradicted text states that 'You will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your representatives.' Since the changed definition of 'You' now excludes indirect subsidiaries, it creates a contradiction regarding which entities are bound by the agreement, making it unclear if indirect subsidiaries' representatives are still bound by the agreement. The original text's broader definition ensured all controlled entities were included, whereas the change narrows the scope, potentially excluding indirect subsidiaries from liability and obligations outlined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection,and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential." The term "Evaluation Materials" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by you or your representatives, (ii) was or becomes available to you on a non-confidential BASIS from a source other than the Company or its representatives, provided that such source is not prohibited from disclosing such information to you by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation to the Company or its representatives, or (iii) is independently developed by you.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Evaluation Materials" includes only information furnished to you in writing and explicitly marked as 'Confidential'. The term "Evaluation Materials" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by you or your representatives, (ii) was or becomes available to you on a non-confidential BASIS from a source other than the Company or its representatives, provided that such source is not prohibited from disclosing such information to you by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation to the Company or its representatives, or (iii) is independently developed by you.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Evaluation Materials' is changed to only include written information explicitly marked as 'Confidential', creating a contradiction with the later section regarding the return of documents. This is a significant alteration, as oral communications and unmarked written material are now excluded from the definition of Evaluation Materials. The In-text Contradiction will be located on the return of documents section, since it has the wording of 'all written Evaluation Materials' while the new pertubation only includes Evaluation Materials as materials being only written and that are marked as confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such material, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Evaluation Materials' to only include written information explicitly marked as 'Confidential'. The contradicted text states that 'all written Evaluation Materials' must be returned or destroyed. Because the changed definition excludes oral information and unmarked written materials from being considered 'Evaluation Materials', this creates a contradiction. The recipient is only obligated to return explicitly marked, written materials according to the changed text, but the contradicted text implies they should return all written evaluation materials. Thus, the changed text directly contradicts the previously established understanding of what constitutes 'Evaluation Materials' in the context of the return obligation.

perturbation.original_text: USE OF EVALUATION MATERIALS You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, <PAGE> representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you, and shall each expressly agree, to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents by writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: USE OF EVALUATION MATERIALS You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the purpose of generally understanding the coal industry. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials confidential only to the extent required by law; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to anyone, and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may be made without the Company's consent.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the permitted use of Evaluation Materials from 'exclusively evaluating a Possible Transaction' to 'generally understanding the coal industry' and weakens the confidentiality requirements. This directly contradicts the earlier definition establishing a requirement of exclusive use for the purpose of a potential acquisition and introduces ambiguity about the level of confidentiality expected. The in-text contradiction is in the first paragraph, in the text is explained what the evaluation materials where for, and that is contradicted by the new evaluation materials use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You (which term shall include your subsidiaries or other entities controlled by you) have requested information regarding Zeigler Coal Holding Company (which term, together with its subsidiaries or other controlled entities, the "Company", "us" or "we") in connection with your consideration of the possible acquisition of the Company (a "Possible Transaction"). In consideration of our furnishing you with the Evaluation Materials (as defined below) you agree as follows:
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the purpose and confidentiality of the Evaluation Materials. The original text states the materials are for 'evaluating a Possible Transaction,' while the changed text broadens this to 'generally understanding the coal industry.' Furthermore, the original text mandates strict confidentiality, whereas the changed text weakens it to 'only to the extent required by law' and allows disclosure to anyone without the Company's consent. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states the information is provided 'in connection with your consideration of the possible acquisition of the Company (a 'Possible Transaction').' The new text allows the use of the evaluation materials for broader purposes that were not agreed, and also allows for less confidentiality, directly contradicting the initial agreement in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Without limiting the generosity of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither you nor your representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting the generosity of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither you nor your representatives shall be liable for any use, unless such use is in direct competition with Zeigler Coal Holding Company.
perturbation.explanation: The clause prohibiting any use of Evaluation Materials if the transaction fails is weakened to only restrict uses that directly compete with Zeigler. This contradicts the earlier stricture against any use after a failed transaction, and also introduces vagueness regarding what constitutes direct competition. The In-text Contradiction is being made against the section defining the term, since in here is establised 'no use' and now with the new pertubation is being established a 'use with limitations'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: USE OF EVALUATION MATERIALS You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, <PAGE> representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you, and shall each expressly agree, to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents by writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that if a Possible Transaction is not consummated, the recipient cannot use the Evaluation Materials for 'any purpose'. The changed text modifies this to allow use unless it is in direct competition with Zeigler Coal Holding Company. This directly contradicts the 'no use for any purpose' clause in the contradicted text. The contradicted text explicitly states 'You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction'. The change introduces a new, limited use case, creating a clear contradiction. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Accordingly, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'unless required by applicable law' creates a contradiction with the understanding that parties are always bound by law, and it is ambiguous if the original clause was intended to prevent disclosure even if legally mandated. This contradicts the general understanding that legal obligations supersede contractual ones, potentially leading to legal conflicts. The In-text Contradiction is with general legal principles, as contracts cannot force parties to break the law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Accordingly, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'unless required by applicable law' from the original text. This creates a direct contradiction because the modified clause now unconditionally prohibits disclosure, even if disclosure is legally mandated. This contradicts the general understanding and legal principle that legal obligations supersede contractual ones. The original clause acknowledges this, while the changed clause does not, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it purports to prevent actions that might be legally required.
